SAAS SUBSCRIPTION AGREEMENT

This SaaS Subscription Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into effective as of
March 1, 2025 ("Effective Date"), by and between:

DataFlow Analytics Inc., a California corporation, with its principal office at
2200 Mission Street, Suite 400, San Francisco, California 94110 ("Provider"), and

TechStartup Inc., a Delaware corporation, with its principal place of business at
1400 Innovation Drive, Suite 200, San Francisco, California 94107 ("Subscriber").

1. SAAS SERVICES

1.1 Subscription Grant. Subject to the terms of this Agreement, Provider hereby
grants to Subscriber a non-exclusive, non-transferable right to access and use
Provider's DataFlow Pro analytics platform (the "Platform") during the Subscription
Term, solely for Subscriber's internal business purposes.

1.2 Users. Subscriber may permit up to fifty (50) authorized users ("Users") to
access the Platform. Subscriber shall ensure that all Users comply with the terms
of this Agreement and shall be responsible for any breach by its Users.

1.3 Service Levels. Provider shall use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain
Platform availability of at least 99.5% measured monthly, excluding scheduled
maintenance windows. Scheduled maintenance shall be performed between 2:00 AM and
6:00 AM Pacific Time on Sundays with at least 48 hours' advance notice.

2. SUBSCRIPTION TERM AND RENEWAL

2.1 Initial Term. The initial subscription term shall commence on the Effective Date
and continue for a period of one (1) year, expiring on February 28, 2026
("Subscription Term").

2.2 Renewal. This Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one (1) year
periods unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal at least thirty
(30) days prior to the expiration of the then-current Subscription Term.

2.3 Effect of Termination. Upon termination or expiration of this Agreement,
Subscriber's right to access the Platform shall immediately cease. Provider shall
make Subscriber's data available for export for a period of thirty (30) days
following termination, after which Provider may delete all Subscriber data.

3. FEES AND PAYMENT

3.1 Subscription Fees. Subscriber shall pay Provider an annual subscription fee of
Seventy-Two Thousand Dollars ($72,000.00), payable in equal monthly installments of
Six Thousand Dollars ($6,000.00) due on the first day of each calendar month.

3.2 Fee Increases. Provider may increase subscription fees upon renewal by providing
at least sixty (60) days' written notice prior to the start of the renewal term,
provided that any increase shall not exceed five percent (5%) of the then-current
fees.

3.3 Payment Terms. All invoices are net thirty (30) days. Overdue amounts shall bear
interest at one percent (1%) per month or the maximum rate permitted by law,
whichever is less.

4. DATA OWNERSHIP AND PRIVACY

4.1 Subscriber Data. As between the parties, Subscriber retains all right, title,
and interest in and to all data submitted to or collected through the Platform by
Subscriber and its Users ("Subscriber Data"). Provider shall not access, use, or
disclose Subscriber Data except as necessary to provide the Platform and related
support services.

4.2 Aggregated Data. Notwithstanding Section 4.1, Provider may collect and use
aggregated, de-identified data derived from Subscriber's use of the Platform for
purposes of improving the Platform, provided that such data cannot be used to
identify Subscriber or any individual.

4.3 Data Processing. Provider shall process Subscriber Data in accordance with its
Data Processing Addendum attached hereto as Exhibit B, which complies with the
requirements of the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and the General Data
Protection Regulation (GDPR) to the extent applicable.

4.4 Data Security. Provider maintains SOC 2 Type II certification and shall
implement and maintain security measures consistent with industry best practices,
including encryption of data in transit (TLS 1.2 or higher) and at rest (AES-256).

5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

5.1 Provider IP. Provider retains all right, title, and interest in and to the
Platform, including all software, algorithms, user interfaces, documentation, and
any improvements or modifications thereto.

5.2 Feedback. If Subscriber provides suggestions, feature requests, or other
feedback regarding the Platform, Provider shall have a royalty-free, worldwide,
perpetual license to use, modify, and incorporate such feedback into the Platform.

6. CONFIDENTIALITY

6.1 Each party shall maintain the confidentiality of the other party's Confidential
Information using the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential
information, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care.

6.2 Confidential Information includes, without limitation, business plans, pricing,
technical specifications, customer lists, and any information marked as confidential.

7. WARRANTIES AND DISCLAIMERS

7.1 Provider warrants that: (a) the Platform will perform materially in accordance
with the applicable documentation; (b) it has the right to grant the licenses
hereunder; and (c) the Platform will not introduce any viruses or malicious code
into Subscriber's systems.

7.2 EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH IN THIS AGREEMENT, THE PLATFORM IS PROVIDED "AS
IS" AND PROVIDER DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING
WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND
NON-INFRINGEMENT.

8. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

8.1 IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL,
CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT.

8.2 EACH PARTY'S TOTAL AGGREGATE LIABILITY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED
THE TOTAL SUBSCRIPTION FEES PAID BY SUBSCRIBER DURING THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS
IMMEDIATELY PRECEDING THE CLAIM.

8.3 THE LIMITATIONS IN THIS SECTION 8 SHALL NOT APPLY TO: (A) EITHER PARTY'S
INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS; (B) PROVIDER'S BREACH OF SECTION 4 (DATA OWNERSHIP
AND PRIVACY); OR (C) EITHER PARTY'S WILLFUL MISCONDUCT.

9. INDEMNIFICATION

9.1 Provider shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Subscriber from any third-
party claim that the Platform infringes any United States patent, copyright, or
trade secret, provided that Subscriber: (a) gives prompt written notice of such
claim; (b) grants Provider sole control of the defense and settlement; and
(c) provides reasonable cooperation at Provider's expense.

9.2 If the Platform becomes, or in Provider's opinion is likely to become, the
subject of an infringement claim, Provider may, at its option: (a) obtain the right
for Subscriber to continue using the Platform; (b) modify the Platform to make it
non-infringing; or (c) terminate this Agreement and refund any prepaid fees for the
unused portion of the Subscription Term.

10. TERMINATION

10.1 Termination for Cause. Either party may terminate this Agreement upon thirty
(30) days' written notice if the other party commits a material breach and fails to
cure such breach within such notice period.

10.2 Termination for Insolvency. Either party may terminate this Agreement
immediately upon written notice if the other party: (a) becomes insolvent; (b) files
or has filed against it a petition in bankruptcy; or (c) makes an assignment for the
benefit of creditors.

11. GOVERNING LAW

11.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws
of the State of California, without regard to its conflict of laws provisions.

11.2 Any legal action or proceeding relating to this Agreement shall be brought
exclusively in the state or federal courts located in San Francisco County,
California, and the parties consent to the personal jurisdiction of such courts.

12. MISCELLANEOUS

12.1 Entire Agreement. This Agreement, including all exhibits and addenda,
constitutes the entire agreement between the parties.

12.2 Waiver. No waiver of any provision shall be effective unless in writing and
signed by the waiving party.

12.3 Notices. All notices shall be in writing and delivered by email with
confirmation of receipt, overnight courier, or certified mail.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the Effective Date.

DATAFLOW ANALYTICS INC.             TECHSTARTUP INC.

By: _________________________       By: _________________________
Name: Priya Sharma                  Name: Sarah Chen
Title: CEO                          Title: CEO
Date: March 1, 2025                 Date: March 1, 2025
